seoTitle: How to Link Within a Document in Google Docs (2026)
metaDescription: Create internal hyperlinks in Google Docs using headings or bookmarks so readers can jump instantly between sections of a long document.
intro: Long documents benefit from internal navigation just as much as web pages do. This guide shows how to link text to headings or bookmarks in Google Docs so readers can jump directly to a specific section with one click. It's a great way to build a functional table of contents or cross-reference system inside a report or thesis.

steps.heading: Select your navigation text
steps.body: Highlight the text block you want to turn into a clickable internal link.

steps.heading: Open the link dialog
steps.body: Press Control+K or Command+K to open the Link panel, which lets you connect your selected text to a destination inside the document.

steps.heading: Choose Headings and bookmarks
steps.body: Expand the Headings and bookmarks section in the link dialog to see a list of available destinations built from your document's structure.

steps.heading: Bind text to a heading
steps.body: Select the target heading to instantly link your text to that section, using your document's existing Heading 1, 2, or 3 structure.

steps.heading: Use bookmarks for finer control
steps.body: For destinations that aren't headings, place a bookmark first, then link text directly to that specific anchor point.

steps.heading: Update or remove links as needed
steps.body: Edit existing internal links to reroute navigation if you restructure your document later, without disrupting your surrounding text.

faq.question: Do I need headings to create internal links?
faq.answer: Not necessarily. Headings make linking easier since they populate the link dialog automatically, but you can also use bookmarks for any location.

faq.question: Can readers click the links to jump around the document?
faq.answer: Yes, once created, internal links function like a clickable table of contents, instantly jumping the reader to the destination section.

faq.question: What happens to my links if I delete a heading?
faq.answer: Links pointing to a deleted heading will break, so you'll need to update or remove them manually after major restructuring.

faq.question: Can I build a full table of contents this way?
faq.answer: Yes, linking multiple headings from an index area is a manual way to build a table of contents, though Google Docs also offers an automated table of contents feature.
